title: Applicant profile model + SQLite store (S6 ranking foundation)
description:
The **applicant-profile model + store** — the Session 6 foundation that ranking/filtering matches opportunities against. Independent of Fetch and of the parse layer. This ticket builds the **model + store only** — **no ranking, no matching, no LLM, no opportunity coupling** (that's a later ticket depending on this + the parse layer). Same shape/precedent as GH-1.

## Design decision for this ticket (v1 shape — locked for now, revisitable)

**Minimal profile + tags.** A small structured core covering the common eligibility gates (location, level, field) plus a list of tags and a free-text bio for future LLM fit-ranking. **Do not over-model demographics** (no citizenship/age/GPA/financial-need matrix in v1) — keep it conservative and easy to extend.

## Locked decisions (honor; see `REPO_CONTENT.md`)

- **Single-user seam:** every row carries `owner`, always `"me"`. **No auth/isolation.**
- **SQLite store**, stdlib `sqlite3`, mirror GH-1's store patterns (configurable db path; tests use a **temporary** db, never a committed db file).
- The applicant profile is the one place applicant data lives; it is distinct from the opportunity store. Keep it minimal.

## Profile fields (v1)

Field | Notes |
---|---|
`id` | stable primary key |
`owner` | defaults `"me"` |
`region` | nullable — location eligibility gate |
`education_level` | nullable — e.g. `"undergraduate"`, `"high school"` |
`field_of_study` | nullable |
`tags` | `list[str]`, defaults empty — interest / eligibility tags (store serializes list <-> text) |
`bio` | nullable free text — the blob a future LLM ranker reads for fit |
`created_at`, `updated_at` | timestamps set on write |

## Scope

1. An `ApplicantProfile` pydantic v2 model with the fields above.
2. A SQLite store: insert / get-by-id / update (single-user typically has one row; keep `list` for symmetry). `tags` round-trips through serialization. Configurable db path; temp db in tests.
3. No ranking, no matching, no opportunity coupling.
acceptance_criteria: `ApplicantProfile` exists with the fields above; `owner` defaults `"me"`; `tags` defaults to `[]`; a profile with every optional field null (except `owner`) constructs validly.
Store insert/get/update round-trips all fields including `tags` (the list survives serialization); db path is configurable; tests use a temporary db.
Unit tests cover construction, store round-trip incl. `tags`, and update. No network, temp db only.
